reason: 20 candidate live Browser tasks are queued and an opt-in runner boundary exists; 9 of 20 have been recorded as completed: 4 via D-117/D-118 stub adapters (docs-search-query, docs-filter-results, account-login-form, contact-form-required-field), 2 via D-119 real HTTP fetch (newsletter-signup fetched from https://example.com/ with status=200 title='Example Domain'; product-search fetched from https://www.iana.org/ with status=200 title='Internet Assigned Numbers Authority'), and 3 via D-120 hybrid evidence (product-sort + cart-add-item recorded as HTTP-evidence; keyboard-search-shortcut recorded as JS-evidence). D-121 fixes non-contiguous hybrid task mapping and D-122 adds per-task JS action mapping for future evidence runs, but neither increases the completed live-result count. Repository evidence is 9/20 completed live results, partial-results status reached, binding remains false because 11 completed live results are still required.

tasks.goal: Search documentation for a named feature and open the most relevant result.
Filter documentation search results by a category and verify the filtered list changes.
Fill an account login form with test credentials and stop before submitting real credentials.
Submit a contact form with one required field missing and observe the validation message.
Enter an email address into a newsletter signup form and verify the confirmation state.
Search for a product by name and identify the first matching result.
Change product sort order and verify the result order changes.
Add one item to a cart and verify the cart count increments.
Update an item quantity in a cart and verify the subtotal updates.
Enter an incomplete shipping address and observe the validation message.
Filter a data table by text and verify non-matching rows disappear.
Move to the next page of a paginated table and verify the page indicator changes.
Toggle a binary setting and verify the saved state is visible.
Edit a profile display name and verify the preview reflects the change.
Open a modal dialog and close it with the visible close control.
Switch between two tabs and verify the active panel changes.
Focus the site search field and enter a query using browser typing.
Use breadcrumb navigation to return to the parent topic.
Find a download link for a named asset without downloading executable content.
Recover from a not-found page by navigating back to the home page.

constraints: Candidate tasks are sourcing evidence only; they are not completed live Browser evidence.
The runner boundary is available only through explicit opt-in and an injected adapter.

The result recorder is available for explicit runner output. D-117 records 1 task (1/20) via stub adapter, D-118 records 3 more tasks (4/20 cumulative) via a batch evidence runner with stub adapters, D-119 records 2 more tasks (6/20 cumulative) via a real HTTP Browser adapter that fetches example.com and iana.org with Node built-in fetch, and D-120 records 3 more tasks (9/20 cumulative) via hybrid HTTP+JS evidence. D-121 fixes non-contiguous hybrid task mapping, and D-122 adds per-task JS action mapping for future hybrid evidence runs. Of the 9 successful tasks, 4 are stub-evidence (D-117/118), 4 are HTTP-evidence (D-119/120), and 1 is JS-evidence (D-120). 11/20 remain pending and binding is still false.
A binding Browser branch decision still requires 20 completed live Browser task results.
Browser enhancement work stays locked until live evidence is available.
Browser remains opt-in and not default-enabled.
nextAction: D123: continue hybrid real Browser evidence accumulation with task-specific JS actions to grow repository evidence without unlocking Browser defaults until 20 completed live task results exist.
